meta_title: For Freelancers: API integration guide to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to API integration guide and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_description: Stop struggling with API integration errors. Follow this expert-led checklist to debug code faster using Antigravity and Claude Code for your next project.
content.answer_block: Debugging API integrations requires a systematic approach: isolate the request, validate the schema, and log the raw response. By using Claude Code for automated analysis and Antigravity for rapid prototyping, you transform cryptic error codes into actionable intelligence, allowing you to ship high-quality client work at maximum velocity.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking. Your client’s project is stalled, the console is bleeding red error messages, and you are trapped in a labyrinth of undocumented API endpoints. That sinking feeling—the one where you realize your integration is failing and you have no idea why—is the silent killer of freelance careers. You don't need to be a senior architect to solve this; you just need a better toolkit.

We are shifting the paradigm today. By combining the rapid-fire logic of Antigravity with the contextual awareness of Claude Code, you will stop guessing and start building. This isn't just about fixing a bug; it is about building a repeatable system that turns you into an indispensable asset for your clients.

steps.title: Isolate the Request Payload
steps.description: Before debugging the logic, ensure the data leaving your app matches the API's expectations exactly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this JSON payload against the provided API documentation and identify any missing required fields or type mismatches.

steps.title: Standardize Error Logging
steps.description: Never guess what the API sent back. Capture the raw headers, status code, and response body into a structured log.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a middleware utility that catches API errors, extracts the error code and message, and formats them into a clean JSON object for debugging.

steps.title: Simulate API Responses
steps.description: Don't rely on live endpoints while debugging. Mock the failure scenarios to trigger your error handling paths.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a set of mock API responses for 400, 401, 403, and 500 status codes based on the current API spec.

steps.title: Refactor Authentication Logic
steps.description: Most integration failures stem from stale tokens or incorrect header authorization. Automate your token refresh flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the authentication header construction and suggest an implementation for an automatic token retry mechanism.

steps.title: Validate against OpenAPI Spec
steps.description: Use the official spec to verify your implementation logic against the source of truth.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Compare my current integration code against the provided OpenAPI yaml file and identify discrepancies in endpoint paths or parameter types.

pro_tips: Always use 'dry-run' flags in your CLI tools to prevent accidental state changes during integration testing.
Implement a 'Circuit Breaker' pattern to prevent your app from hammering an API that is already returning 500s.
Store your API keys in environment-specific secrets managers, never in the codebase, even during local testing.
Use Claude Code to generate unit tests based on your API response logs to ensure the bug never returns.
Keep a 'cURL' library of successful requests; it is the fastest way to confirm if a bug is in your code or the API provider.
content.next_steps: Once your integration is stable, move to automating the monitoring of these endpoints using a lightweight uptime checker to proactively alert you before your client notices a failure.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting API errors dictate your freelance schedule. Master these tools, scale your output, and deliver professional-grade integrations every single time. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
